title: Genome editing enhancement
abstract: This invention pertains to single-stranded carrier nucleic acids and their methods of use for enhancing genome editing ribonucleoprotein complex transfection into cells and the resulting enhancement of CRISPR editing on the target DNA within those cells, as well as introduction of chemical modifications which reduce the integration of the single-stranded carrier nucleic acids at double-stranded breaks.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A carrier oligonucleotide to improve transfection of ribonucleoprotein (RNP) into one or more cells in a sample, the carrier oligonucleotide comprising a single-stranded sequence not homologous to a cell sequence in a genome of the cell, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length greater than 20 nucleotides. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the concentration of the carrier oligonucleotide introduced to the sample is at least 1 Î¼Î. 3. The composition of claim 1, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 4. 4. A carrier oligonucleotide to improve transfection of Cpfl ribonucleoprotein (RNP) into one or more cells in a sample, the carrier oligonucleotide comprising a single-stranded oligonucleotide optionally containing a hairpin, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is not homologous to a cell sequence in a genome of the cell and the carrier oligonucleotide is a length is greater than 20 nucleotides. 5. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of 60 to 150 nucleotides. 6. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of 100 to 125 nucleotides. 7. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of at least 112 nucleotides. 8. The composition according to any of claims 4-7, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide further comprises at least three abasic spacers. 9. The composition according to claim 8, wherein the at least three abasic spacers are selected from the group consisting of C3, S9 and dSpacer. 10. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 9 or SEQ ID No. 25. 11. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 9. 12. The composition of claim 4, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 25. 13. A method of improving transfection of a ribonucleoprotein into a cell of a sample, comprising: contacting the cell of the sample with composition having a carrier oligonucleotide comprising a single-stranded sequence not homologous to a cell sequence in a genome of the cell, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length greater than 20 nucleotides. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the concentration of the carrier oligonucleotide in the composition is at least 1 Î¼Î. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 4. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the ribonucleoprotein comprises a Cpfl rib onucl eoprotein . 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of 60 to 150 nucleotides. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of 100 to 125 nucleotides. 19. The method of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide is a length of at least 112 nucleotides. 20. The method according to any of claims 13-19, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide further comprises at least three abasic spacers. 21. The method according to claim 20, wherein the at least three abasic spacers are selected from the group consisting of C3, S9 and dSpacer. 22. The composition of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 9 or SEQ ID No. 25. 23. The composition of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 9. 24. The composition of claim 16, wherein the carrier oligonucleotide comprises SEQ ID No. 25.
